report_name: "Living Within SaaS": Cordial & Snarky Spider Groups Use Vishing, SSO Abuse for Rapid Extortion

threat_actors.name: Cordial Spider
threat_actors.description: An emerging cybercrime group linked to 'The Com' ecosystem, conducting rapid data theft and extortion campaigns against U.S. organizations. It uses vishing and AiTM phishing to compromise SSO credentials and move laterally within victim SaaS environments.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for financial extortion, with ransom demands often reaching seven figures.

threat_actors.name: Snarky Spider
threat_actors.description: An emerging cybercrime group linked to 'The Com' ecosystem, working alongside Cordial Spider. It specializes in vishing and SSO abuse to gain access to enterprise SaaS applications for data exfiltration and extortion.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for financial extortion, with ransom demands often reaching seven figures.

threat_actors.name: The Com
threat_actors.description: A notorious e-crime collective to which Cordial Spider and Snarky Spider are linked. It serves as an ecosystem for these extortion-focused threat actors.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through various cybercrime activities.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used as the primary initial access vector through social engineering, including vishing calls, texts, or emails to impersonate IT support.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: The groups conduct vishing calls, impersonating IT help desk staff to direct victims to malicious SSO portals.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
attack_patterns.description: Adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing sites were used to capture not just credentials but also session cookies and MFA tokens in real-time.

attack_patterns.name: Modify Authentication Process: Multi-Factor Authentication
attack_patterns.description: After gaining access, the attackers were observed disabling MFA for the compromised account to solidify their foothold and evade defenses.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors deleted security alerts within SaaS platforms to avoid detection after compromising an account.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: With compromised SSO credentials, the attackers pivoted to connected SaaS applications like SharePoint, Salesforce, and Google Drive to perform discovery and access sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: High-value data was exfiltrated from victim SaaS environments for extortion purposes.

attack_patterns.name: Proxy
attack_patterns.description: The groups used residential proxy networks to obscure their location and blend in with normal traffic, making IP-based blocking ineffective.

tools.name: Mullvad
tools.description: Used as a residential proxy network to anonymize attacker traffic and bypass geolocation-based security controls.

tools.name: Oxylabs
tools.description: Used as a residential proxy network to anonymize attacker traffic and bypass geolocation-based security controls.

tools.name: Infatica
tools.description: Used as a residential proxy network to anonymize attacker traffic and bypass geolocation-based security controls.

tools.name: 9Proxy
tools.description: Mentioned as a residential proxy service whose IP ranges should be monitored for anomalous SaaS logins.

identities.name: U.S.-based organizations
identities.description: The primary targets of Cordial Spider and Snarky Spider's extortion campaigns, spanning multiple critical sectors in the United States.

infrastructure.name: AiTM Phishing Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing sites that perfectly mimic an organization's SSO portal to capture credentials, session cookies, and MFA tokens.

indicators.name: Anomalous SaaS Login From Residential Proxy
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous SaaS Login From Residential Proxy
id: 2b1a9c8d-f3e4-4b5a-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects successful logins to cloud or SaaS platforms originating from IP ranges associated with residential proxy services (e.g., Mullvad, Oxylabs, Infatica).
  This activity is a key TTP of groups like Cordial Spider and Snarky Spider for accessing compromised accounts.
  This rule is conceptual and requires a reliable IP address feed for the specified proxy services.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/cybercrime-groups-using-vishing-and.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-02
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.t1090
level: medium
logsource:
  category: cloud
  service: sso
detection:
  selection:
    event_type: 'sso_login'
    outcome: 'success'
    # The 'source.ip_reputation.category' field is a placeholder for a field enriched by a threat intelligence provider.
    source.ip_reputation.category: 'residential_proxy'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects logins to SaaS platforms originating from known residential proxy IP ranges (e.g., Mullvad, Oxylabs, Infatica). This is a hunting hint provided in the article to detect activity related to Cordial Spider and Snarky Spider.

indicators.name: User-Initiated MFA Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: User-Initiated MFA Modification
id: 5e8f7a6b-4c3d-4e2f-8a1b-0c9d8e7f6a5b
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects when a user de-registers or modifies their Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) methods.
  This can be a defense evasion technique used by attackers like Cordial Spider after an account is compromised to weaken security.
  This alert should be correlated with other suspicious activity, such as a recent login from an anomalous location.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/cybercrime-groups-using-vishing-and.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-02
tags:
  - attack.t1556.006
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: auditlogs
detection:
  selection:
    OperationName|startswith: 'User registered security info'
    OperationName|endswith: ' deleted'
    Result: 'success'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Alerts on any user-initiated MFA de-registration or re-enrollment, a key defense evasion tactic mentioned in the report.

indicators.name: SaaS Security Alert Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: SaaS Security Alert Deletion
id: 9c8d7b6a-5f4e-4d3c-b2a1-e0f9d8c7b6a5
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the deletion of security alerts within a SaaS platform like Microsoft 365.
  This is a defense evasion technique used by threat actors to cover their tracks after gaining access.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/cybercrime-groups-using-vishing-and.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-02
tags:
  - attack.t1562.008
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.general
detection:
  selection:
    Operation: 'Remove-ProtectionAlert'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Monitors audit logs in platforms like Microsoft 365 for the deletion of security alerts, a tactic used by the threat actors to hide their presence.

relationships.target_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
